title: B.Sc. (Hons with Research) in Mathematics with Specialization in Computational Mathematics
content: In addition to its core mathematical rigor, the B.Sc. Mathematics (Honours with Research) is a four year degree program which offers a specialized stream in Computational Mathematics, which equips students with the skills to solve complex mathematical problems using algorithmic and numerical methods. This stream includes courses such as Computational Mathematics, Advanced Numerical Techniques, Data Structures and Algorithms, Machine Learning, Cloud Computing, and Blockchain Technologies, ensuring students are proficient in both theory and application. With hands-on exposure to programming languages like Python and tools like MATLAB or Scilab, students develop the capability to model, simulate, and analyze real-world systems. The integration of computational mathematics enhances problem-solving efficiency and prepares graduates for cutting-edge careers in data analytics, software development, quantitative finance, and scientific research. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057) score will be given preference in the merit list 01 This research-focused program enables students to innovate in computational mathematics, tackling advanced equations and algorithms through original theses, performance benchmarking, and conference-ready outputs for cutting-edge scientific computing. 01 02 03 04 05 06 07 08 01 Graduates will establish careers in academic research or high-performance computing, authoring 2+ publications on computational algorithms or numerical analysis. 02 Graduates will develop novel computational models for scientific simulations, securing funding or patents through collaborative research. 03 Graduates will lead research initiatives in areas like scientific computing or AI, presenting at international forums with impactful results. 04 Graduates will pursue PhDs or senior R&D roles, exhibiting expertise in advanced topics like parallel computing and fostering innovation in mathematics applications. 01 Mathematical Foundations: Understanding core concepts, principles, and techniques in computing and related mathematical foundations, enabling effective problem-solving, algorithm development, and application of computing solutions across diverse domains. 02 Computational Techniques: Apply computational methods and numerical algorithms using tools like Scilab, Python, R etc. to solve mathematical problems that arise in science, engineering, and technology. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057) score will be given preference in the merit list
